subject: HSC English Advanced
description: Index of marking guidelines by year and module. Each entry provides band descriptors, general marking principles, and question-level guidance aligned with NESA criteria. English Advanced is assessed holistically against performance bands — there is no single correct answer. The marking-guideline-feedback skill should use these descriptors to give criterion-referenced feedback.
importantNote: Unlike Mathematics, English Advanced responses cannot be marked as correct or incorrect. All marking is holistic and criterion-referenced against band descriptors. Feedback should: (1) identify which band the response is performing in and why, (2) name specific strengths, (3) give concrete, actionable advice for moving to the next band.

partialCreditPolicy.description: English Advanced partial credit and band assessment policy, aligned with NESA principles

rules.condition: Strong analytical argument but limited textual evidence
rules.marksAwarded: Middle band — evidence integration is essential for upper bands

rules.condition: Excellent evidence but analysis does not develop a sustained argument
rules.marksAwarded: Middle band — argument cohesion required for upper bands

rules.condition: Narrative or descriptive response rather than analytical
rules.marksAwarded: Lower band — must demonstrate analytical, not descriptive, engagement

rules.condition: Addresses the question partially (e.g. only affirm, not challenge)
rules.marksAwarded: Cap at middle band — full response to the question required for upper bands

rules.condition: Creative piece strong but reflective statement absent or minimal
rules.marksAwarded: Module C: deduct up to 3 marks from total; reflective statement is separately assessed

rules.condition: Module A: texts treated separately, not comparatively
rules.marksAwarded: Cap at lower-middle band — integration is a requirement, not an option

bandDescriptors.description: General NESA band descriptors for HSC English Advanced extended responses (out of 20). Applied proportionally to shorter questions.

Band6.descriptor: The student demonstrates extensive and detailed knowledge and understanding of the text(s) and the module/concept. They make sophisticated, original and sustained judgements, perceptively analysing how language forms, features and structures shape meaning. They express well-developed, cohesive and insightful arguments with clarity, precision and fluency.

Band5.descriptor: The student demonstrates thorough knowledge and understanding of the text(s) and the module/concept. They make well-developed and sustained judgements, analysing how language forms, features and structures shape meaning with competence. They express clear, well-organised arguments with control and fluency.

Band4.descriptor: The student demonstrates sound knowledge and understanding of the text(s) and the module/concept. They make relevant judgements and show some analytical engagement with language forms, features and structures. Arguments are present but may be uneven, partially developed, or inconsistently supported.

Band3.descriptor: The student demonstrates some knowledge of the text(s) and the module/concept. Analysis is attempted but may be largely descriptive or superficial. Evidence is used but the connection between technique and meaning is underdeveloped. Expression may affect clarity.

Band2.descriptor: The student demonstrates limited knowledge of the text(s) or the module/concept. The response is largely narrative or descriptive. Little or no analytical engagement with language. Evidence is minimal or absent.

analytical: Reward analytical engagement with language, form and structure — not plot summary or general commentary
A response that demonstrates a genuine, original reading of the text should be rewarded even if it is unconventional
Evidence must be integrated — a response with excellent argument but no textual evidence cannot exceed Band 4
Metalanguage should be precise and purposeful — naming a technique earns nothing unless its effect is explained
Sophisticated responses explore complexity, tension, and paradox — not just surface meaning
essayStructure: A clear, arguable thesis is essential for Band 5–6 — 'This text explores human experiences' is not a thesis
Every paragraph must advance the argument — descriptive paragraphs cap the response at Band 3–4
Conclusion must synthesise, not repeat — a conclusion that mirrors the introduction suggests limited analytical range
Responses that address only part of the question are capped — read carefully and answer fully

CommonModule: Responses must engage explicitly with the module concept of 'texts and human experiences'
The individual/collective distinction is central — responses that address only individual experience are incomplete
Section I unseen responses must be grounded in the specific unseen text — generic technique lists without evidence score Band 2–3
ModuleA: Integration of both texts throughout is mandatory — separated analysis is capped at Band 3–4
Context must be embedded in analysis, not listed as background information
The thesis must make a claim about the RELATIONSHIP between texts, not just what each explores
ModuleB: Responses must evaluate, not merely analyse — Module B requires genuine critical judgement
Textual integrity must be demonstrated through specific close language analysis
A personal, authentic critical voice is rewarded — generic or encyclopaedic responses score Band 3–4
ModuleC: Creative pieces are assessed on craft, not on content — avoid formulaic or predictable responses
The reflective statement is separately assessed (3 marks) — a missing reflective statement loses those marks entirely
A literal response to the stimulus scores in Band 2–3 — sophisticated responses find unexpected angles

generalPrinciples: Holistic assessment against band descriptors — there is no single correct interpretation
Reward analytical engagement with language, not description of content
Evidence must be embedded and precise — long block quotes with no analysis are penalised
Expression and coherence of argument contribute to the band assessment
Module-specific requirements must be met — e.g. integration in Module A, evaluation in Module B

paper1.description: Paper 1 — Common Module: Texts and Human Experiences

sectionI.description: Unseen texts — short answer and extended response
generalNotes: Responses must be grounded in the specific unseen texts provided
Short answer responses: mark allocation determines depth required — 1 mark per focused analytical point
Extended response: assess against band descriptors scaled to 8 marks
Reward specific, precise textual engagement over general thematic discussion
A response that does not address the specific question asked is capped at Band 3

paper2.description: Paper 2 — Modules A, B and C
generalNotes: Each module assessed at 20 marks using module-specific band descriptors
Module C: 17 marks for creative piece + 3 marks for reflective statement

2023-EA-P1-Q1a.keySkill: technique identification and effect explanation using metalanguage

2023-EA-P1-Q1b.keySkill: analysis of collective experience through language and structure

2023-EA-P1-Q1c.keySkill: sustained analysis across texts with integration of form

2023-EA-P1-Q2.keySkill: comparative analysis of form across two unseen texts

2023-EA-P1-Q3.keySkill: sustained analytical essay on prescribed text with thesis and close language analysis

2023-EA-P2-Q4.keySkill: integrated comparative essay with contextual analysis

2023-EA-P2-Q5.keySkill: evaluative essay demonstrating textual integrity and critical perspective

2023-EA-P2-Q6.keySkill: crafted creative piece (17 marks) + metacognitive reflective statement (3 marks)

generalPrinciples: Holistic assessment against band descriptors — there is no single correct interpretation
Reward analytical engagement with language, not description of content
Evidence must be embedded and precise — long block quotes with no analysis are penalised
Expression and coherence of argument contribute to the band assessment
Module-specific requirements must be met

2022-EA-P2-Q5.keySkill: evaluative essay on literary value and textual integrity
